title: Jatropha transiens
taxonomy: Jatropha transiens is classified as a species within the plant family Euphorbiaceae. This family is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as the spurges. The genus Jatropha is a group of flowering plants native to the Americas and Africa, and Jatropha transiens is a specific species within this group. Jatropha transiens is further classified as a member of the subgenus Jatropha, which includes species with similar characteristics. The classification of Jatropha transiens is used to identify and distinguish it from other species within the Euphorbiaceae family.

intro: Jatropha transiens is a species of flowering plant in the family Euphorbiaceae, native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiparasitic activities. The bioactive constituents of Jatropha transiens include flavonoids, terpenoids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant include decoctions and infusions, as well as extracts in the form of capsules and tinctures, although its use should be approached with caution due to potential toxicity.
